entries.main_competence: 1.0 Manage information search from different sources for lifelong learning
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Organise information obtained from different sources
entries.learning_activity: (a) Identify different ways/strategies for organising information from various sources
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through searching for different ways/ strategies for organising information from various sources and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: Strategies for organising information from various sources are identified
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Manage information search from different sources for lifelong learning
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Organise information obtained from different sources
entries.learning_activity: (c) Compile information searched from various sources
teaching_learning_activities: Project: Assign the students a project work to search for information from different sources
I do, we do, you do: Guide the students through using the “I do, we do, you do” method to compile the information searched on various sources
entries.assessment: Information searched from various sources is compiled properly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (a) Hold conversations about various contexts using appropriate grammar and vocabulary (structure: tenses, adverbs, adjectives, active and passive voice, conjunctions, coordinators, prepositions)
teaching_learning_activities: Scenario: Give the students a scenario to share ideas in groups using conversation techniques, appropriate grammar and vocabulary
Debates: Guide the students through developing topics for conversations and participating in debates on the topics
Interviews: Guide the students through preparing and participating in community interviews about community needs, concerns and expectations
Telephoning: Guide the students through making telephone conversations on various topics using appropriate grammar and vocabulary
entries.assessment: Conversations about various contexts using appropriate grammar and vocabulary are held
resources: audio and audio - visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (b) Compose stories using proper grammar and vocabulary (structure: tenses, adverbs, adjectives, active and passive voice, conjunctions, coordinators, prepositions)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming ideas on the main parts of stories and reading stories with appreciation
Story mapping : Guide the students through planning the structure of stories using a story map graphic organiser.
Composition: Guide the students through using their story plans to compose stories using proper grammar and vocabulary and sharing the stories for discussion
entries.assessment: Stories using proper grammar and vocabulary are composed
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe events (e.g., general elections, state funerals, world cup) using correct grammar and vocabulary
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming ideas on the main issues to include when describing provided events and presenting them to the class
Talk show: Guide the students through preparing scripts for talk shows to describe events and participating in the talk shows
Description: Guide the students through describing events orally and in writing using correct grammar and vocabulary
entries.assessment: Events are described by using correct grammar and vocabulary
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe events (e.g., general elections, state funerals, world cup) using correct grammar and vocabulary
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming ideas on the main issues to include when describing provided events and presenting them to the class
Talk show: Guide the students through preparing scripts for talk shows to describe events and participating in the talk shows
Description: Guide the students through describing events orally and in writing using correct grammar and vocabulary
entries.assessment: Events are described by using correct grammar and vocabulary
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension
entries.learning_activity: (b) Express connotative meanings of words in relation to a text
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing and providing examples of connotative meanings of words in relation to the texts read, interpreting such meanings and presenting the work to the class
entries.assessment: Connotative meanings of words in relation to a text are expressed appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
entries.learning_activity: (a) Discuss information from various oral sources
teaching_learning_activities: Discussions: Read passages aloud and play recorded information. Then, guide the students through discussing the information heard and presenting the work to the class
Interview: Guide the students in pairs or groups through participating in interviews on topical issues
Project work: Ask the students to listen to and watch various oral sources (radio, TV), discuss the information heard, and present their work to the class
entries.assessment: Information from various oral sources is discussed appropriately
resources: audio and audio-visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
entries.learning_activity: (b) Respond to various oral messages
teaching_learning_activities: Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplay to respond to oral instructions (e.g., instruction manual and computer application ), announcements and advertisements
Interviews: Guide the students through preparing and participating in field specific interviews (e.g., journalism, sports, and politics)
entries.assessment: Various oral messages are responded to accordingly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Express ideas, opinions and facts in mass communication contexts
teaching_learning_activities: Talk show: Guide the students through TV or radio talk show to express their ideas, opinions and facts on a controversial or opinion topic

Discussion: Guide the students through discussing a controversial or opinion topic by expressing their ideas, opinions and facts in writing and publishing their works on social media or in the school magazine
entries.assessment: Opinions and facts in mass communication are expressed clearly
resources: audio and audio-visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
entries.learning_activity: (e) Express appreciation of other people’s ideas in public communication
teaching_learning_activities: Gallery walk: Guide the students in groups through gallery walk to discuss and present words/expressions used in appreciating other people’s ideas
Dialogue: Guide the students through preparing and acting out a dialogue whose participants appreciate each other’s ideas
entries.assessment: Appreciations of other people’s ideas in public communication are expressed suitably
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.4 Construct meaning from a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Compare ideas from various texts
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming how to compare ideas from various texts and presenting their work to the class
Close reading : Provide the students with various texts to read and guide them through comparing ideas from the texts read
entries.assessment: Ideas from various texts are compared correctly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.4 Construct meaning from a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Read and generate meanings from a variety of texts
teaching_learning_activities: Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through identifying different types of meaning and presenting their works to the class
Discussion: Guide the students through reading a variety of texts, discussing different meanings contained in the texts and presenting their works to the class
entries.assessment: Meanings from a variety of texts are generated
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.4 Construct meaning from a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Paraphrase textual information from multiple sources
teaching_learning_activities: I do, we do, you do:
Modelling: Show the students how to paraphrase textual information from multiple sources using different techniques
Shared practice: Collaborate with the students to paraphrase textual information from multiple sources using different techniques
Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through paraphrasing textual information from multiple sources using different techniques
entries.assessment: Textual information from multiple sources is paraphrased properly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Express opinions through debates, dialogues and impromptu speeches using formulaic language (e.g., In my opinion…, personally…, I think …, I would say…, In my view…, from my perspective…, My point is…, It seems to me that…, I believe…)
teaching_learning_activities: Debate: Guide the students through using formulaic language to give their opinions in debates
Dialogue: Guide the students through reading aloud and acting out a dialogue with an opinion topic
Delivering impromptu speeches: Guide the students through delivering impromptu speeches on opinion topics using formulaic language
entries.assessment: Opinions are expressed through debates, dialogues, and impromptu speeches using formulaic language
resources: recorded materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories) on various situations
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe conditions about various situations and circumstances (structure: conditional sentences)
teaching_learning_activities: Investigation : Guide the students through investigating the structure of conditional sentences and presenting the work to the class
Chain conditionals (Conditional train): Guide the students through constructing conditional sentences orally using chain conditionals
Pelmanism (Concentration) game: Guide the students through playing the pelmanism game to construct conditional sentences orally
Mill drill questionnaire (Mingling activities)
Guide the students through constructing conditional sentences orally using the mill drill questionnaire
Group discussion: Assign the students roles to play (e.g., politicians, teachers, traffic police) and provide them with the same questions to discuss based on the particular roles
Songs: Guide the students through singing songs with conditionals and identifying the conditionals in the songs
entries.assessment: Conditions about various situations and circumstances are described appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Ask and respond to questions in interview sessions (e.g., job interview, panel interview, TV/radio interview)
teaching_learning_activities: Modelling : Use recorded interview sessions on different topics to model students on how to ask and respond to interview questions
Interview/roleplay: Guide the students through engaging in interviews by roleplaying the role of interviewees and interviewers
entries.assessment: Interview questions are asked and responded to correctly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (a) Write a letter to the editor, an order letter and a complaint letter (structure: Dear editor.., I would like to order…. We are glad to place our first order…, I am writing to complain about…, I am writing to bring your attention…, a complaint against…)
teaching_learning_activities: Observation and discussion: Guide the students through studying letters to the editor, order letters and complaint letters and discussing important considerations when writing such letters

Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through writing letters to the editor, order letters and complaint letters using appropriate grammar and vocabulary
entries.assessment: Letter to the editor, order and complaint letters are written correctly
resources: samples of letter to the editor
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (c) Write biographies (structure: past and present tense, third person)
teaching_learning_activities: Biographies: Guide the students through reading and summarising biographies of famous people in writing
Biography graphic organisers: Guide the students through the use of biography graphic organisers to write biographies of famous people
entries.assessment: Biographies are written correctly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 5.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 5.1 Apply principles of editing and proofreading in a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Recognise the basic principles of editing and proofreading texts (e.g., sentence construction, language clarity, grammar, semantic rules, logical flow)
teaching_learning_activities: Snowball discussions: Guide the students through discussing the basic principles of editing and proofreading using snowball discussions
entries.assessment: The basic principles of editing and proofreading are recognised
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 5.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 5.1 Apply principles of editing and proofreading in a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Use the principles of editing and proofreading to edit and proofread simple and short texts in a school context (e.g., laboratory rules, announcements)
teaching_learning_activities: Modelling: Engage the students by showing them how to edit and proofread texts
Shared practice: Work with the students to edit and proofread a text
Collaborative and independent practice: Provide the students in pairs and individually with simple and short texts in the school context and guide them through editing and proofreading the texts
entries.assessment: The principles of editing and proofreading simple texts in the school context are used appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 5.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 5.2 Apply principles of interpretation to provide simple authentic interpretations
entries.learning_activity: (b) Elaborate the principles of interpretation
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through searching for information about the principles of interpretation and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The principles of interpretation are elaborated precisely
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 5.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 5.3 Apply principles of translation to produce simple authentic translation
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of translation
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students in groups through brainstorming the concept of translation and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The concept of translation is explained correctly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 5.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 5.3 Apply principles of translation to produce simple authentic translation
entries.learning_activity: (b) Elaborate the principles of translation
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing the principles of translation and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The principles of translation are elaborated correctly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.1 Appreciate the aesthetics and value of literature
entries.learning_activity: (a) Assess the language used in selected poems and plays
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students in groups through brainstorming the language used in poems and plays, and presenting their work to the class
Shared practice: Select a poem and a play, and collaborate with the students to assess the language used
Collaborative and independent practice: Provide the students in pairs/groups and individually with poems and plays to read and guide them through assessing the language used
entries.assessment: The language used in selected poems and plays is assessed appropriately.
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.1 Appreciate the aesthetics and value of literature
entries.learning_activity: (a) Assess the language used in selected poems and plays
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students in groups through brainstorming the language used in poems and plays, and presenting their work to the class
Shared practice: Select a poem and a play, and collaborate with the students to assess the language used
Collaborative and independent practice: Provide the students in pairs/groups and individually with poems and plays to read and guide them through assessing the language used
entries.assessment: The language used in selected poems and plays is assessed appropriately.
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.1 Appreciate the aesthetics and value of literature
entries.learning_activity: (b) Compare own values and perspectives with those implied in selected poems and plays
teaching_learning_activities: TIPS: Guide the students through identifying own values and perspectives using TIPS
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through comparing own values and perspectives with those implied in the selected poems and plays, and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: Own values and perspectives are compared appropriately with those implied in selected poems and plays
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.2 Evaluate the context in which literary texts are written, read and understood
entries.learning_activity: (a) Assess the socio-political and cultural contexts in which the selected poems and plays were written
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through searching for information on the techniques to identify the setting of a poem and presenting their work to the class
Group work: Guide the students through assessing the socio-political and cultural contexts of the selected poems and plays
entries.assessment: The socio-political and cultural contexts in which the selected poems and plays were written are assessed appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.2 Evaluate the context in which literary texts are written, read and understood
entries.learning_activity: (c) Relate the context of the story in selected poems and plays with real life experiences
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students in groups through brainstorming ideas on real-life experiences

Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through identifying the context of the story in the selected poems and plays, relating such context with their life experiences and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The context of the story in selected poems and plays is related properly with real-life experiences
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.3 Analyse genres of literature and appreciate their conventions
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe different genres of written literature and their conventions
teaching_learning_activities: Two Truths and a Lie: Guide the students through identifying genres of written literature through two truths and a lie
Discussion/Group work : Guide the students in groups through describing different genres of written literature and their conversations. Then, guide each group through presenting its work to the class
entries.assessment: Different genres of literature and their conventions are described correctly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.3 Analyse genres of literature and appreciate their conventions
entries.learning_activity: (b) Analyse techniques of critiquing poems and plays based on their conventions
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through searching for information about the techniques for critiquing poems and plays based on their conventions
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through sharing the information searched about the techniques for critiquing poems and plays, analysing the techniques and presenting them to the class
entries.assessment: Techniques for critiquing poems and plays based on their conventions are analysed appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.3 Analyse genres of literature and appreciate their conventions
entries.learning_activity: (c) Critique selected poems and plays based on form and content
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students in pairs through brainstorming the elements of form and content in a literary work and presenting their work to the class
Group work : Guide the students in groups critiquing poems and plays based on form and content
entries.assessment: Poems and plays are clearly critiqued based on form and content
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.4 Create simple literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Compose free verse poems on various topics using literary language
teaching_learning_activities: Think-aloud-pair problem solving: Guide the students through Think-aloud-pair problem solving to explain steps in composing a poem
Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through composing free verse poems on various topics using literary language
entries.assessment: Topics for composing poems are identified appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.4 Create simple literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Compose free verse poems on various topics using literary language
teaching_learning_activities: Think-aloud-pair problem solving: Guide the students through Think-aloud-pair problem solving to explain steps in composing a poem
Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through composing free verse poems on various topics using literary language
entries.assessment: Topics for composing poems are identified appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 6.4 Create simple literary works
entries.learning_activity: (b) Publish the poems on various platforms (e.g., note boards and online platforms)
teaching_learning_activities: Publishing: Guide the students through publishing their poems composed on various platforms (e.g., note boards, or on the Internet)
entries.assessment: Poems are published properly on various platforms
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)
